obligations.reason: Debug DM slave interface (dm_slave_req/we/addr/wdata/rdata) provides read/write access to on‑chip resources. The design contains a jtag_unlock signal and a jtag_hash register suggesting debug authentication exists. Without verifying that every dm_slave transaction is gated by jtag_unlock, a debugger might bypass the authentication sequence and directly access protected assets.
obligations.subject: Debug module (i_SimDTM / i_dmi_jtag) through dm_slave interface
obligations.operation: Read and write to system memory / peripherals via dm_slave_req, dm_slave_we, dm_slave_addr, dm_slave_wdata, dm_slave_rdata
obligations.object: Protected assets reachable through the debug slave port (e.g., bootrom, CLINT, AES, SHA256, HMAC, DMA configuration, PMP entries)
obligations.expected_guard: dm_slave_req must not be asserted unless jtag_unlock == 1 (active) and the debug state machine has completed a valid authentication handshake.
obligations.candidate_path: driving logic of dm_slave_req, dm_slave_we, and dm_slave_addr; connection from jtag_unlock / jtag_hash to dmi_jtag / SimDTM; any bypass path via testmode_i or debug_mode_i that sets dm_slave_req independent of jtag_unlock.
state_conditions_to_check: conditions under which jtag_unlock is set / cleared
whether testmode_i can override the gating of dm_slave_req
whether debug_mode_i forces dm_slave enable irrespective of jtag_unlock
required_evidence_slots: path/reachability (from TAP to dm_slave_req without unlock)
obligations.uncertainty: The precise condition for gating dm_slave_req by jtag_unlock is not visible in the fact layer; must be confirmed in RTL.

obligations.reason: Bootrom access is gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0] (privilege level). The design also inputs debug_mode_i. If debug_mode_i is asserted, it might override priv_lvl_i or bypass the acc_ctrl_c check, allowing a debugger to read sensitive bootrom content without the expected privilege.
obligations.subject: Debug state (debug_mode_i) or any requester that can set debug_mode_i
obligations.operation: Read bootrom data (rom_rdata) via the bootrom request path
obligations.expected_guard: rom_req (rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]) must not be forced high when debug_mode_i is active and priv_lvl_i does not satisfy the required privilege; or there must be a parallel guard on the read mux that prevents debug from exposing rom_rdata.
obligations.candidate_path: Assignment 'rom_req = rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]'; any override of priv_lvl_i by debug_mode_i; any mux that routes rom_rdata to debug response without rom_req being active.
state_conditions_to_check: Effect of debug_mode_i on priv_lvl_i and rom_req_acct
Existence of a debug read path to rom_rdata that bypasses rom_req
obligations.uncertainty: Unclear whether debug_mode_i directly influences rom_req_acct or priv_lvl_i; must be verified in RTL.

obligations.reason: The register lock control (reglk_ctrl) is present to protect peripheral register writes. The DMA wrapper contains core_lock_reg and accepts reglk_ctrl_i. If any write path to DMA configuration (start, length, source/dest addresses) exists that is not guarded by core_lock_reg (or the lock state is incorrectly implemented), an attacker could reprogram the DMA even after locks are set, leading to unauthorized memory transfers.
obligations.subject: Any bus master (CPU via AXI, debug interface via dm_slave) writing to DMA configuration registers
obligations.operation: Write to DMA start, length, source_addr_lsb/msb, dest_addr_lsb/msb registers
obligations.object: DMA configuration registers (start_reg, length_reg, etc.) inside dma_wrapper / dma
obligations.expected_guard: The write enable for these registers must be qualified by (core_lock_reg == UNLOCKED) or an equivalent lock check; the lock bit itself must be sticky and not writable after being set (except by a privileged unlock sequence).
obligations.candidate_path: Write strobe generation for start_reg, length_reg, etc. in dma_wrapper; core_lock_reg setting/clearing logic; any alternative write path via dm_slave to the same addresses that does not check core_lock_reg.
state_conditions_to_check: Reset value of core_lock_reg (should be UNLOCKED or LOCKED as per spec)
Whether dm_slave writes to DMA registers are gated by core_lock_reg
Whether testmode_i can override the lock
obligations.uncertainty: The exact locking semantics (sticky, writable once) and coverage of all DMA registers need to be checked.

obligations.reason: The access-control vector acc_ctrl_c is computed with an OR combination for index j==5: 'acc_ctrl_c[i][j] = acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i])'. This may grant bits from a lower-index peripheral to peripheral 5, potentially escalating privileges. For example, if a less privileged master controls acc_ctrl for peripheral 4, that bit will also be set for peripheral 5, granting unintentional access.
obligations.subject: Any software or hardware that writes to the acc_ctrl register affecting peripheral 4
obligations.operation: Write to acc_ctrl bits corresponding to peripheral 4
obligations.object: Access control bits for peripheral 5 (used by acc_ctrl_c to gate access to a protected resource)
obligations.expected_guard: The permission for peripheral 5 should depend solely on its own acc_ctrl bits, not be logically ORed with any other peripheral's bits. The equation should not include 'j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]'.
obligations.candidate_path: The continuous assignment of acc_ctrl_c, specifically the term (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]), and any use of acc_ctrl_c[j==5][i] in gating sensitive resources.
state_conditions_to_check: Value of acc_ctrl[4*4+i] when peripheral 5 is expected to be locked
Which resource is protected by acc_ctrl_c[j==5][i]
required_evidence_slots: expected guard (no OR with other index)
observed behavior (acc_ctrl_c[j==5][i] set when acc_ctrl[4*4+i] is set)
obligations.uncertainty: Need to confirm whether the OR combination was intentional (e.g., peripheral 5 is a superset of 4) or a design error; impact depends on what peripheral 5 protects.

obligations.reason: The DMA engine includes a PMP (Physical Memory Protection) check via pmp_addr_reg, pmp_access_type_en, pmp_allow_reg, and the pmp module. A typical vulnerability occurs when the PMP configuration can be changed while an active DMA transfer is using it, allowing a transfer to proceed with stale or unauthorized permissions. Also, the debug or test path must not be able to bypass the PMP check.
obligations.subject: DMA transaction state machine or debug/test interface
obligations.operation: DMA memory read/write or debug access to memory through DMA bypass
obligations.object: Memory regions whose access is restricted by PMP rules
obligations.expected_guard: A DMA transfer must not be launched or must be aborted if the PMP allow signal is de‑asserted; the PMP configuration registers (pmp_addr_reg, pmp_access_type_en) must be locked while a transfer is in progress; debug/test must not circumvent the allow check.
obligations.candidate_path: Logic between pmp_allow_reg (or pmp_data_allow wire) and the DMA transfer valid signal; enable signals for pmp_addr_reg and pmp_access_type_en in relation to transfer active state; any debug path that forces valid or sets pmp_allow_reg.
state_conditions_to_check: Race between pmp configuration update and active transfer (pmp_check_ctr_en, valid_en, etc.)
Whether debug can set pmp_allow_reg directly or through debug write to DMA registers
required_evidence_slots: expected guard (PMP allow and config stability)
observed behavior (transfer proceeds without re‑checking allow or after config change)
state conditions (transfer active, config write strobes)
obligations.uncertainty: The interaction between DSC and PMP may be complex; evidence must be gathered from the RTL signals listed.

obligations.reason: The testmode_i input is a global signal that can put the chip in a manufacturing test mode. In many designs, testmode disables all security guards (locks, access controls, PMP). The fact layer shows testmode_i is an input to riscv_peripherals. It must be verified that testmode_i does not silently bypass reglk_ctrl, acc_ctrl_c, jtag_unlock, or PMP checks, and that it cannot be asserted after production (e.g., requires a physical strap or is disabled by fuse).
obligations.subject: Test controller logic reacting to testmode_i
obligations.operation: Activation of testmode (asserting testmode_i) and subsequent access to protected resources
obligations.object: All protected assets (bootrom, crypto, DMA, memory, lock bits)
obligations.expected_guard: testmode_i must not override security-critical signals such as dm_slave_req enable, rom_req, DMA lock, reglk_ctrl, or PMP allow; if it does, the testmode must be permanently disabled in secure lifecycle states.
obligations.candidate_path: Any combinatorial block or conditional that uses testmode_i to gate or override write enables, access control signals, or reset lock bits.
state_conditions_to_check: How testmode_i is used in riscv_peripherals and submodules
Whether any lock signal (reglk_ctrl, core_lock_reg) is forced to unlocked state under testmode
Lifecycle or fuse that can disable testmode
required_evidence_slots: expected guard (no override of security signals)
obligations.uncertainty: testmode_i fanout is not visible in the provided fact layer; must be inspected in the full RTL.

evidence_slots.subject: Debug slave interface (i_dmi_jtag / i_dm_top) via dm_slave_req
evidence_slots.operation: Read/write requests through dm_slave_req, dm_slave_we, dm_slave_addr, dm_slave_wdata, dm_slave_rdata
evidence_slots.object: Debug module registers; indirectly system memory/peripherals accessible via hart debug control
evidence_slots.expected_guard: dm_slave_req must be gated by jtag_unlock == 1; only authenticated debug sessions should assert the slave request
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_slave_req is driven directly from i_dm_axi2mem (line 406) into i_dm_top (line 367) without any qualification by jtag_unlock; jtag_unlock is connected only to i_reglk_wrapper (line 1820) and does not appear in the dm_slave path
evidence_slots.path: dm_master (from i_debug_axilite_bridge) → i_dm_axi2mem → dm_slave_req → i_dm_top.slave_req_i; jtag_unlock from i_dmi_jtag → jtag_unlock wire → i_reglk_wrapper only
evidence_slots.state_condition: jtag_unlock is produced by i_dmi_jtag but never used to gate the DM slave interface; testmode_i is input to i_dmi_jtag and i_dm_top but no visible override in riscv_peripherals.sv
evidence_slots.impact: A debugger can access the debug module slave port and control harts without completing the authentication handshake, bypassing the entire jtag_unlock security mechanism

rtl_evidence.description: Declaration of jtag_unlock signal
jtag_unlock_o connection from i_dmi_jtag
Declaration of dm_slave_req signal
Connection of dm_slave_req to i_dm_top slave_req_i
i_dm_axi2mem drives dm_slave_req directly from dm_master
Sole consumer of jtag_unlock is reglk_wrapper, no gating on DM slave interface

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Exact internal use of testmode_i within i_dmi_jtag and i_dm_top not visible; full verification of jtag_unlock generation is needed.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Critical violation: dm_slave_req is completely unguarded by jtag_unlock; any JTAG/DTM activity can access debug module without authentication.
inspection_records.notes: Debug DM slave port is a direct path to hart control; this finding requires immediate remediation by inserting a gate using jtag_unlock before asserting dm_slave_req.

evidence_slots.subject: debug_mode_i input signal
evidence_slots.operation: Potential override of bootrom read access control
evidence_slots.expected_guard: rom_req must remain strictly gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]; debug_mode_i must not modify priv_lvl_i or rom_req_acct to bypass
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: debug_mode_i is not referenced in the bootrom access path; rom_req = rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0] (line 517) uses only priv_lvl_i and acc_ctrl_c; debug_mode_i fans out to crypto/rst wrappers but not to bootrom; dm_master is tied off, preventing debug-initiated AXI reads
evidence_slots.path: NoC → i_bootrom_axilite_bridge → br_master → i_axi2rom → rom_req_acct → AND with acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0] → rom_req; debug_mode_i has no connection to this path
evidence_slots.state_condition: debug_mode_i does not influence priv_lvl_i or rom_req_acct in any visible logic; dm_master is tied to zero, so debug cannot issue AXI reads to bootrom address
evidence_slots.impact: No bypass identified; bootrom access control remains robust against debug_mode

rtl_evidence.description: rom_req assignment with acc_ctrl_c gate
debug_mode_i declaration
dm_master tie-off (gnt, r_valid, r_rdata = 0)
debug_mode_i consumers; none in bootrom path

inspection_records.notes: Bootrom access control is correctly isolated from debug_mode_i.

evidence_slots.subject: Any bus master writing to DMA configuration registers via AXI
evidence_slots.operation: Write to start_reg, length_reg, source_addr_lsb_reg, source_addr_msb_reg, dest_addr_lsb_reg, dest_addr_msb_reg, done_reg
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Writes must be gated by core_lock_reg == UNLOCKED; core_lock_reg must be sticky and not re-writable while locked
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: All DMA register writes are qualified only by en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i (line 92) and en && we (line 117); core_lock_reg is not in the write-enable chain. Furthermore, core_lock_reg write logic (line 129) is not sticky: (wdata==0) ? 0 : ((core_lock_reg==0) ? wdata : 0) allows unlocking by any non-zero write.
evidence_slots.path: axi_lite_interface → en_acct → en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i → en && we → register write; core_lock_reg is a separately addressed register with no influence on other registers
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset: core_lock_reg = 0 (unlocked); lock can be cleared by writing any non-zero value when already locked; reglk_ctrl_i port (line 46) is declared but never used in dma_wrapper
evidence_slots.impact: DMA configuration registers are freely writable regardless of lock state; an attacker can reprogram DMA transfers even after software believes the DMA is locked

rtl_evidence.description: en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i, not using core_lock_reg
Write enable gate: en && we
Non-sticky core_lock_reg write: unlocks on any non-zero write
reglk_ctrl_i input declared but unused inside module

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Whether reglk_ctrl_i from riscv_peripherals is intended to serve as lock; currently unconnected. No testmode influence on DMA locks detected.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Critical: core_lock_reg provides no protection; DMA can be reprogrammed at any time. The lock implementation is fundamentally broken.
inspection_records.notes: Must redesign lock: make core_lock_reg sticky (write-once to lock, ignore subsequent writes), and add core_lock_reg condition to the en signal for all configuration registers.

evidence_slots.subject: Software/hardware writing to acc_ctrl bits of peripheral 4 (HMAC)
evidence_slots.object: Effective access control for peripheral 5 (PKT) via acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5]
evidence_slots.expected_guard: acc_ctrl_c[i][5] must depend solely on acc_ctrl[5*4+i]; no OR with any other peripheral's bits
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Line 222: assign acc_ctrl_c[i][j] = acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]); therefore acc_ctrl_c[i][5] = acc_ctrl[5*4+i] | acc_ctrl[4*4+i]. acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5] is used by i_pkt_wrapper (line 1627).
evidence_slots.path: Continuous assignment in generate block (line 222) → acc_ctrl_c → i_pkt_wrapper.acct_ctrl_i → access control for PKT peripheral
evidence_slots.state_condition: Whenever acc_ctrl[4*4+i] is set, peripheral 5's effective access control bit is also set, regardless of acc_ctrl[5*4+i] value
evidence_slots.impact: Escalation of privilege: granting access to HMAC (peripheral 4) automatically grants the same privilege-level access to PKT (peripheral 5); can compromise PKT (fuse/security asset) protection

rtl_evidence.description: acc_ctrl_c assignment with OR of peripheral 4 bits for j==5
i_pkt_wrapper uses acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5] as access control input

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Intent of OR combination unclear; whether it is a bug or deliberate shared access for PKT requires design spec confirmation.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: High-severity: privilege escalation via inadvertent sharing of access control bits due to a coding error.
inspection_records.notes: The OR term (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]) should be removed unless explicitly documented and justified.

evidence_slots.subject: DMA PMP check FSM (CTRL_CHECK_LOAD / CTRL_CHECK_STORE) and debug/test interfaces
evidence_slots.operation: DMA memory read/write accesses with PMP enforcement
evidence_slots.object: Memory regions restricted by PMP rules
evidence_slots.expected_guard: PMP allow must be continuously re-evaluated per transaction; no unconditional bypass; PMP configuration must not change during active transfer; debug/test must not override allow
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: In CHECK_LOAD state (line 203-220): pmp_allow_new = (pmp_allow_reg && (pmp_data_allow || (pmp_check_ctr_reg == length_d))) || (we_flag && length_d == 3). The term (we_flag && length_d == 3) unconditionally sets allow when length is 3 and we_flag is high, which could be used to bypass PMP for specific workloads. In CTRL_LOAD state (line 244-256): pmp_allow_new = 1 is set unconditionally before moving to CHECK_STORE. This means once load is committed, PMP allow is forced to 1 for the store phase, negating store-side PMP checks. No locking of PMP configuration registers during transfer; they are external inputs wired directly to the pmp module.
evidence_slots.path: dma.sv: FSM states CHECK_LOAD, CTRL_LOAD, CHECK_STORE → pmp_allow_new/pmp_allow_reg → valid generation; external pmpcfg_i/pmpaddr_i directly connect to pmp module
evidence_slots.state_condition: we_flag = 1 and length_d = 3 can override PMP allow in load check; CTRL_LOAD unconditionally sets pmp_allow to 1, enabling unchecked store phase; PMP config can change at any time as no lock logic
evidence_slots.impact: Potential PMP bypass: DMA can perform unauthorized memory accesses by exploiting unconditional allow or length=3 we_flag path; store phase runs without PMP re-validation

rtl_evidence.description: CHECK_LOAD state with (we_flag && length_d == 3) bypass and unconditional allow logic
CTRL_LOAD sets pmp_allow_new = 1 unconditionally
PMP configuration registers directly updated from external inputs without locking
pmpcfg_i/pmpaddr_i passed through to dma module without lock or gating

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Exact scenario where we_flag is asserted; whether it is a legitimate test mode or can be abused. No evidence of PMP config locking during transfer.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: High risk: DMA PMP enforcement is weakened by an unconditional allow path and lacks configuration stability during transfer; can lead to violation of memory protection policies.
inspection_records.notes: Recommend locking PMP config registers during DMA transfer and removing unconditional allow paths.

evidence_slots.subject: testmode_i global input
evidence_slots.object: All protected assets (bootrom, crypto, DMA, lock bits)
evidence_slots.expected_guard: testmode_i must not override any security-critical signals; if it does, it must be disabled in secure lifecycle states via fuse or physical strap
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: testmode_i is connected to i_dmi_jtag (line 278), i_dm_top (line 361), and i_clint (line 613). It is NOT connected to bootrom logic, DMA wrapper, reglk_ctrl, or acc_ctrl assignments in visible RTL. No evidence of testmode overriding locks or access controls in riscv_peripherals.sv.
evidence_slots.path: testmode_i input → i_dmi_jtag, i_dm_top, i_clint (direct port connections); no further fanout found in riscv_peripherals
evidence_slots.state_condition: No usage of testmode_i to gate acc_ctrl_c, rom_req, dm_slave_req, or lock registers in riscv_peripherals; impact limited to submodules
evidence_slots.impact: Within the visible periphery, testmode does not directly bypass security; however, internal behavior of dmi_jtag and dm_top under testmode is unknown and could provide indirect bypass. Without lifecycle/fuse control, testmode remains a potential risk if those submodules weaken security in test mode.

rtl_evidence.description: testmode_i input declaration
testmode_i connected to i_dmi_jtag
testmode_i connected to i_dm_top
testmode_i connected to i_clint
testmode_i not used in dma_wrapper, dma, or any lock/access control logic

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Internal logic of i_dmi_jtag, i_dm_top, and i_clint under testmode is not available; lifecycle/efuse control is not verified.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: No direct bypass observed in riscv_peripherals, but risk remains due to unknown submodule behavior. Full verification requires analyzing submodules and confirming testmode disable in production.
inspection_records.notes: Investigate dmi_jtag and dm_top testmode handling; ensure testmode cannot be entered after production via fuse or physical strap.
